title: 10 best herbal teas for laryngitis
intro: For laryngitis, herbal teas can provide soothing relief for irritated throats. Slippery elm tea is a popular choice due to its demulcent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and coat the throat. Licorice root tea is another option, as it has a natural sweetness and anti-inflammatory effects. Marshmallow root tea, rich in mucilage, can also help soothe inflammation and moisturize the throat. Additionally, ginger tea with its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties can offer comfort. However, it is crucial to consult a healthcare provider if symptoms persist for more than one week, as laryngitis can sometimes be a sign of a more severe condition.

teas.tea_desc: Slippery elm tea provides relief for laryngitis due to its demulcent property, meaning it has a softening and soothing effect. This tree's inner bark contains mucilage, a substance that becomes a slick gel when mixed with water. By coating the throat's lining, this gel provides a barrier against irritants and reduces inflammation. Furthermore, it alleviates pain and irritation while promoting healing in the affected tissues. Slippery elm tea stands out as a time-honored natural remedy for laryngitis, approved by the German Commission E for treating inflammation of the mouth and throat.
tea_parts: Bark: The slippery elm's inner bark is loaded with mucilage, a substance that becomes a slick gel when mixed with water. This gel coats and soothes inflamed mucous membranes, making it perfect for laryngitis relief.
Leaves: Slippery elm leaves are also rich in mucilage. When steeped in hot water, they release this soothing substance, providing a natural remedy for laryngitis-related irritation.

Seeds: While not as mucilaginous as the bark or leaves, slippery elm seeds still contain some of this beneficial slimy substance. They can be used to make tea and assist in alleviating laryngitis symptoms.

Roots: Although less commonly used than the bark or leaves, slippery elm roots can also be employed to make medicinal tea. They contain mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect inflamed tissues in the throat.

teas.tea_desc: Marshmallow tea brings immediate relief to laryngitis due to the demulcent property of its main ingredient, Althaea officinalis. This plant-based substance creates a protective film over the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation. As a result, soreness in the throat significantly decreases, and the natural hydrating effect of marshmallow tea soothes dryness. Furthermore, it promotes expectoration of phlegm, enabling clearer breathing and improved vocal cord function. Ultimately, marshmallow tea's anti-inflammatory and healing qualities address the discomfort and symptoms of laryngitis effectively.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, marshmallow leaves soothe irritated throat tissues due to laryngitis.
Flowers: Abundant in mucilage, marshmallow flowers coat and protect inflamed mucous membranes, providing relief from laryngitis.
Roots: High in antioxidants, marshmallow roots boost the immune system, helping the body fight laryngitis.
Bark: Containing healing polysaccharides, marshmallow bark forms a protective layer over throat tissues, alleviating symptoms of laryngitis.

teas.tea_desc: Licorice tea brings immediate relief to laryngitis due to its demulcent properties, which involve a film-forming ability that coats the throat's mucous membrane. This tea's primary ingredient, glycyrrhizin, exhibits anti-inflammatory qualities, reducing swelling in the throat and alleviating discomfort. Furthermore, licorice tea soothes irritation by increasing the mucus's production, ensuring a protective lubricant layer over the affected area. Additionally, this natural remedy encourages the removal of phlegm, making it easier to expel mucus and other foreign particles. Lastly, the warm temperature of licorice tea increases blood circulation in the throat, enhancing the immune system's response to inflammation and infection.
tea_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, the roots of the licorice plant soothe inflamed mucous membranes, providing relief for laryngitis.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and volatile oils, licorice leaves promote respiratory health, easing laryngitis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant are a natural source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, benefiting those suffering from laryngitis.
Seeds: Loaded with beneficial phytochemicals, licorice seeds support a healthy immune response, alleviating laryngitis symptoms.

teas.tea_desc: Chamomile tea brings relief to laryngitis due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the throat. This herbal tea's soothing warmth eases discomfort by calming irritation. Chamomile's rich antioxidant content strengthens the immune system, helping the body fight laryngitis-causing infections. Furthermore, chamomile tea's mild sedative effects promote restful sleep, aiding in recovery. Its mucolytic action thins and loosens mucus, making it easier to expel.
tea_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, chamomile flowers produce a soothing tea that helps alleviate irritation in the throat, a common symptom of laryngitis.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, chamomile leaves create a comforting tea that helps reduce inflammation in the larynx, providing relief from laryngitis.
Seeds: Packed with anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile seeds produce a potent tea beneficial for treating laryngitis and soothing the related discomfort.

teas.tea_desc: Peppermint tea brings immediate relief to laryngitis due to its active component, menthol, which possesses strong antiviral and antibacterial properties. This component works by forming a soothing barrier over the larynx, reducing inflammation and irritation. Moreover, peppermint tea stimulates blood flow, accelerating the healing process. Its natural anti-inflammatory agents further alleviate pain and swelling in the throat. Additionally, peppermint tea liquefies mucus, making it easier to expel, and inhibits the growth of harmful microorganisms causing the infection.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol, the leaves offer effective cough suppressant and analgesic properties, soothing the inflamed larynx.
Flowers: Containing rosmarinic acid, flowers provide strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping alleviate laryngitis symptoms.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, seeds offer potent antimicrobial properties, aiding in fighting infections causing laryngitis.

teas.tea_desc: Thyme tea provides relief for laryngitis due to its well-documented antibacterial and antiviral properties, derived from its active compounds, namely thymol and carvacrol. These compounds actively combat infection-causing agents, reducing inflammation and soothing irritation in the throat. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and clear the airways. Furthermore, thyme tea's antioxidant qualities strengthen the immune system, promoting faster recovery. Consequently, these properties position thyme tea as an effective natural remedy for laryngitis symptoms, providing both soothing relief and combating the root cause of the ailment.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves of thyme are commonly used to soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in the throat associated with laryngitis.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, thyme flowers help protect the throat tissue from damage, supporting quicker recovery from laryngitis.
Stems: Containing essential oils, thyme stems offer antibacterial properties, aiding in eliminating the cause of laryngitis and promoting faster healing.

teas.tea_desc: Sage tea provides relief for laryngitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which reduce swelling in the throat and alleviate coughing spasms. The rosmarinic acid in sage tea inhibits the production of substances that cause inflammation, while its soothing effect coats the throat, providing immediate comfort. Furthermore, sage tea's antibacterial properties help fight off any potential infection contributing to laryngitis, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, sage tea's astringent qualities help tighten and tone throat tissues, reducing irritation and discomfort.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves help soothe inflamed tissues in the throat.
Flowers: High in antioxidants, flowers aid in reducing irritation and promoting healing.
Aerial parts: While not a specific part, it's worth noting that aerial parts of some plants have medicinal value; however, they are excluded from this list.
Stems: Containing essential oils, stems help alleviate congestion and ease breathing.
Roots: Packed with therapeutic properties, roots provide relief from pain and reduce inflammation caused by laryngitis.

teas.tea_desc: Echinacea tea contains active compounds, such as alkamides and caffeic acid, that possess anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. These properties help reduce swelling in the larynx, thereby alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with laryngitis. Echinacea also has antiviral and antibacterial effects, which aid in fighting off infections causing laryngitis. Furthermore, it stimulates the production of white blood cells, enhancing the body's ability to combat illness. Lastly, echinacea tea soothes irritated throats by providing a warm and comforting sensation, while its mild analgesic qualities help minimize the need for over-the-counter pain relievers.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of the echinacea plant contain beneficial compounds that help bolster the immune system, making it effective in soothing laryngitis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the echinacea plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce irritation in the throat, providing relief from laryngitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the echinacea plant are rich in antioxidants that fight off infections, making them useful for treating laryngitis.
Buds: The buds of the echinacea plant contain high levels of therapeutic compounds that can alleviate soreness and irritation in the throat.
Bark: (not a part of the echinacea plant used in medicinal teas)

teas.tea_desc: Goldenseal tea helps with laryngitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. This herbal tea contains berberine, a compound known to reduce inflammation in the mucous membranes, including the larynx. Moreover, goldenseal tea's antibacterial qualities help fight off infections causing laryngitis, thus promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, this tea soothes sore throats by creating a protective coating on the mucous membranes. Lastly, goldenseal tea boosts the immune system, increasing the body's ability to combat the laryngitis-causing ailment.
tea_parts: Roots: Goldenseal roots contain berberine, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe irritated throat tissues.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of goldenseal plants are rich in alkaloids, offering antibacterial and immune-boosting benefits, which aid in fighting laryngitis-causing infections.
Leaves: Goldenseal leaves have anticatarrhal actions, helping to reduce excess mucus production and alleviating the symptoms of laryngitis.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, goldenseal flowers provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, supporting overall throat health during laryngitis.

teas.tea_desc: Turmeric tea provides relief from laryngitis due to its active ingredient, curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound works by inhibiting the production of inflammation-causing molecules, thus reducing swelling in the throat. Furthermore, curcumin's antibacterial qualities combat infection, a common cause of laryngitis. Turmeric tea also soothes irritation, creating a protective barrier in the throat. Additionally, this warming beverage helps loosen mucus, promoting its elimination and alleviating congestion.
tea_parts: Rhizomes: These are the horizontal underground stems of the turmeric plant that are rich in curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in soothing irritation in the throat caused by laryngitis.

Leaves: The leaves of the turmeric plant contain therapeutic compounds that have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory ailments, including laryngitis, due to its expectorant and soothing properties.

Roots: The turmeric roots, or tubers, are used in medicinal teas for laryngitis because of their potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat.

Flowers: The flowers of the turmeric plant contain bioactive compounds that have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory infections and reduce inflammation, making it a suitable addition to medicinal teas for laryngitis.
